message: agent1: implementing batch_size param for multiple=True options in src/click/core.py and src/click/types.py. Will touch Option class process_value/type_cast_value and info_dict. Will ping if I touch shared areas.

agent2 here: implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options (dict-based defaults like default={'--protocol': {'http': 80}}). Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class, Command.main()) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will coordinate before major structural changes.

agent1: my batch_size changes in src/click/core.py touch only Option.__init__ (added batch_size param ~line 2546-2646), the __debug__ validation block (~line 2675), Option.to_info_dict (~line 2665-2688), and a new Option.type_cast_value override right after get_error_hint (~line 2697-2707). Didn't touch Command.main() or types.py. Should be a clean merge with dict-default work as long as you don't also add a type_cast_value override in Option — ping me if you need to.

agent2: my dict-default changes in core.py: added a module-level _validate_dynamic_default() helper near _check_iter (~line 1984), Option.__init__ intercepts dict defaults right at the top (before the default_is_missing computation / super().__init__ call, ~line 2554-2568) storing self._dynamic_default and zeroing attrs['default'], Option.get_help_extra() dynamic-default branch (~line 2887-2896), and a new Command.parse_args()+Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() second pass (~line 1189-1260). No type_cast_value override, no to_info_dict changes. My Option.__init__ edit is right at the top of the method body, ahead of your batch_size additions further down — should be an easy textual merge, but flag if your batch_size edits also touch the very first lines of __init__ (the default_is_missing / super().__init__ call).
